definition: The 95% confidence interval on U.S. fiscal multiplier estimates spans 0.2 to 2.5, driven by identification strategy, sample period, business cycle phase, and monetary offset. This uncertainty is itself a first-order finding that dominates any specific point estimate. Multipliers are state-contingent (~1.5-2.5x at ZLB/recession, ~0.0-0.5x during expansion with monetary offset).
evidence: SVAR identification: 0.5-1.0 (1Y); narrative identification: 1.0-1.5 spending, 2.5-3.0 tax
IMV coefficient shrinks ~40% for monetary-sovereign issuers
No robust nonlinear threshold in panel data with country fixed effects
Current regime (6-7% deficit, sub-4% unemployment, Fed on hold) has no historical precedent
open_questions: What is the correct functional form at extreme deficit levels (steady-state 6-7%)?
Does deficit composition (transfers vs. procurement vs. interest) produce measurably different multipliers?
What is the fiscal consolidation speed-severity tradeoff (fast vs. slow cuts)?
How do multipliers interact with the current monetary policy stance (on hold, not easing)?
